name: Jiloca River Bank to Torralba de Sisones & Bello - circular from Calamocha
name_original: Ribera del Jiloca hasta Torralba de Sisones & Bello - circular desde Calamocha
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Calamocha traverse the extensive plain of the Jiloca River valley, offering diverse landscapes for cyclists. The region features a mix of rolling hills, reforested European pine trees, and rocky steppes. Cyclists can expect varied terrain, including both paved and unpaved sections, providing different challenges. The area's natural features, such as the Jiloca River and its tributary the Pancrudo River, shape many of the available routes. Best road cycling routes around Calamocha The most popular road cycling route is Jiloca River Bank to Torralba de Sisones & Bello - circular from Calamocha, a 32.0 miles (51.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route follows the Jiloca River bank and passes through rural villages. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Barrachina – Calamocha loop from Calamocha-Nueva, a moderate 42.6 miles (68.6 km) path. This loop offers a mix of open countryside and gentle ascents. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de Fonfría (1470 m) – Calamocha loop from Calamocha-Nueva, a 57.0 miles (91.8 km) trail leading through elevated terrain and a mountain pass, often completed in about 5 hours 11 minutes. Road cycling around Calamocha is defined by the Jiloca River valley, rolling hills, and open steppes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Calamocha are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 190 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Calamocha's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Calamocha?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Calamocha on komoot, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy rides to challenging tours, with 15 routes classified as difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Calamocha?
A: Road cycling routes in Calamocha primarily traverse the extensive plain of the Jiloca River valley. You can expect diverse landscapes, including rolling hills, reforested European pine trees, and rocky steppes. While the guide focuses on road cycling, the region also features some unpaved sections on other routes, offering varied challenges.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in the Calamocha area?
A: Yes, the region offers family-friendly cycling options, particularly along the Via Verde de Ojos Negros. This greenway, following an old mining railway line, is generally downhill and provides an enjoyable and accessible experience for families. A section from Calamocha to Cella covers approximately 58.8 km.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while cycling around Calamocha?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical elements. You can explore the scenic Jiloca River valley, which shapes many routes. A unique natural phenomenon, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5088573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento&lt;/a&gt;, is a spring where water continuously falls in drops, forming a spectacular karst relief. Additionally, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman bridge of Luco de Jiloca&lt;/a&gt;, adding a historical touch to your ride.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Fonfría (1470 m) – Calamocha loop from Calamocha-Nueva&lt;/a&gt; offer elevated terrain and mountain passes, often taking over 5 hours to complete. Other difficult routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10418429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños del Segura – Puerto de Rudilla loop from Calamocha-Nueva&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain over 127 km.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Calamocha?
A: While specific seasonal weather patterns are not detailed, the diverse terrain suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling. Summers can be hot in Aragon, and winters might bring colder conditions, so checking local forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Calamocha?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Calamocha are designed as loops. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968435990/jiloca-river-bank-to-torralba-de-sisones-bello-circular-from-calamocha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jiloca River Bank to Torralba de Sisones & Bello - circular from Calamocha&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 51.5 km loop that follows the Jiloca River bank and passes through rural villages.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Calamocha?
A: The road cycling routes in Calamocha are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Over 190 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes originating from Calamocha?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer expeditions, routes like the historic 'Calamocha-Sagunto' span over 235 km. While not detailed in this guide, the region's network supports extensive tours for endurance cyclists.

Q: Are there any specific cycling paths or greenways in the area?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4592106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santander–Mediterranean Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is a notable cycling path in the vicinity. Additionally, the Via Verde de Ojos Negros, Spain's longest greenway, is easily accessible and offers a generally downhill gradient, making it ideal for cycle tourism.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes around Calamocha?
A: The road cycling routes around Calamocha offer a range of difficulty levels. You'll find 1 easy route, 7 moderate routes, and 15 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners and experienced cyclists alike.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the Jiloca River valley?
A: Many routes in Calamocha are shaped by the Jiloca River valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968435990/jiloca-river-bank-to-torralba-de-sisones-bello-circular-from-calamocha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jiloca River Bank to Torralba de Sisones & Bello - circular from Calamocha&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, following the river's banks and offering scenic views of the valley.
tips: A city of transit since ancient times, Calamocha stands in the middle of the extensive plain of the Jiloca, on the right bank of the river. Its excellent geographical situation led to its being an important fortified enclave in the past. In fact, it was the city of El Cid passing through on his way to Valencian lands.
Small or not so small, town on the Camino del Cid. The hostel is highly recommended, both for accommodation and for food. We also have shops, fountains and a pharmacy.

Torralba de los Sisones is located in the Jiloca region, just south of the Gallocanta lagoon. In the town, the ruins of the castle of San Pedro, of Muslim origin, stand out, as well as the church of the same name, a 16th century construction with a beautiful bell tower. It also has a hostel with a restaurant.
Town that is located next to the Gallocanta Lagoon and where it is characterized by having a "skyline" with a high tower that is currently a hostel, but with impressive views of the plain.
